perturbation.original_text: 3.5. ȊConfidential informationȋ means any information of whatever form relating to the Project or Discloser or any of its Affiliates or Clients, supplied or made available by Discloser or on its behalf to recipient or Recipient Representatives, copies of any such information regardless of whether such information is identified as confidential or not; and information regarding: 3.5.1. The existence of the Project; 3.5.2. The identity of the Discloser and the willingness of the Discloser to enter into discussions and/or negotiations regarding the Project; 3.5.3. Any information including those parts of analyses, compilations, studies and other documents which contain, reflect or are derived from such information referred to in this Clause 3.4 or discussions and negotiations relating to the project.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5. ȊConfidential informationȋ means information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Discloser at the time of disclosure. Information not explicitly marked as such is not considered Confidential Information under this agreement; and information regarding: 3.5.1. The existence of the Project; 3.5.2. The identity of the Discloser and the willingness of the Discloser to enter into discussions and/or negotiations regarding the Project; 3.5.3. Any information including those parts of analyses, compilations, studies and other documents which contain, reflect or are derived from such information referred to in this Clause 3.4 or discussions and negotiations relating to the project.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' includes 'any information of whatever form...regardless of whether such information is identified as confidential or not.' The modified definition restricts it to only information 'explicitly marked as Confidential.' This contradicts the original all-encompassing definition, particularly regarding information derived from analyses or discussions related to the project, creating uncertainty as to what qualifies as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5. ȊConfidential informationȋ means information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Discloser at the time of disclosure. Information not explicitly marked as such is not considered Confidential Information under this agreement; and information regarding: 3.5.1. The existence of the Project; 3.5.2. The identity of the Discloser and the willingness of the Discloser to enter into discussions and/or negotiations regarding the Project; 3.5.3. Any information including those parts of analyses, compilations, studies and other documents which contain, reflect or are derived from such information referred to in this Clause 3.4 or discussions and negotiations relating to the project.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including information regardless of whether it's explicitly marked as confidential. The changed text narrows this definition to only information 'explicitly marked as Confidential.' The contradicted text, identical to the changed text, reinforces this narrowed definition. The initial broad definition conflicts with the subsequent restricted definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction. If a recipient received confidential information that wasn't explicitly marked, it would have been considered confidential under the original clause 3.5, but is no longer confidential under the changed clause 3.5. This introduces an inconsistency in what is considered confidential.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipientȇs work in relation to the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipient’s work in relation to the Project. However, Recipient may use anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly prohibits copying or distributing Confidential Information except to essential Representatives. The added sentence allows the Recipient to use 'anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes.' This directly contradicts the prohibition on copying and distributing, even if it's anonymized, as the anonymization process itself likely involves reproduction. This ambiguity creates uncertainty about the scope of permitted use and could lead to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipient’s work in relation to the Project. However, Recipient may use anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original clause (5.1.3) strictly prohibits any copying or distribution of Confidential Information, allowing access only to essential Representatives. The modified clause introduces an exception, permitting the use of 'anonymized and aggregated Confidential Information for internal research purposes.' This exception directly contradicts the initial prohibition, even if the information is anonymized, as the anonymization process itself involves copying and potentially distributing the data within the recipient's organization. The explanation states that the anonymization process likely involves reproduction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the addition of the new clause contradicts the first half of the clause. Therefore, this creates a clear in-text contradiction as the modified text permits an action that was previously forbidden.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2. Recipient shall be liable for its Representatives' acts and omissions as if such acts or omissions had been its own acts or omissions, even when such persons/entities cease to be (as the case may be) employee, director, senior executive, professional advisor, consultant, Affiliate or supplier of any tier in relation to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2. Recipient shall be liable for its Representatives' acts and omissions as if such acts or omissions had been its own acts or omissions, even when such persons/entities cease to be (as the case may be) employee, director, senior executive, professional advisor, consultant, Affiliate or supplier of any tier in relation to Recipient. However, Recipient will not be liable for Representatives’ acts if the Representative acted outside the scope of their engagement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'Recipient shall be liable for its Representatives' acts and omissions as if such acts or omissions had been its own acts or omissions, even when such persons/entities cease to be...in relation to Recipient.' The modification introduces an exception: 'Recipient will not be liable for Representatives’ acts if the Representative acted outside the scope of their engagement.' This creates a direct contradiction, as the original text implies liability regardless of scope, while the addition provides a loophole. This introduces ambiguity regarding the Recipient’s liability, especially in scenarios where a Representative's actions are ambiguous or disputed as being within the scope of engagement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2. Recipient shall be liable for its Representatives' acts and omissions as if such acts or omissions had been its own acts or omissions, even when such persons/entities cease to be (as the case may be) employee, director, senior executive, professional advisor, consultant, Affiliate or supplier of any tier in relation to Recipient. However, Recipient will not be liable for Representatives’ acts if the Representative acted outside the scope of their engagement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient shall be liable for its Representatives' acts and omissions, without exceptions, even after the representative leaves their position. The changed text introduces an exception where the Recipient is NOT liable if the Representative acted outside the scope of their engagement. This new exception creates a direct contradiction by limiting the scope of liability previously established without limits. Therefore, based on the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1.1. This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations for any of the Parties to this Agreement, to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project, and any Party can cease to continue such discussions at any time. The Agreement does not grant Recipient any exclusivity, and Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1.1. This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations for any of the Parties to this Agreement, to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project, and any Party can cease to continue such discussions at any time. The Agreement does grant Recipient a right of first refusal for any related projects undertaken during the term of this agreement, however The Agreement does not grant Recipient any exclusivity, and Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement explicitly states that 'This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations...to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project'. The altered agreement adds 'The Agreement does grant Recipient a right of first refusal for any related projects undertaken during the term of this agreement' introducing a direct contradiction, as the first statement denies any obligation, while the second confers a specific right of first refusal, creating uncertainty in legal enforceability of the contract due to such in-text contradictions
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1.1. This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations for any of the Parties to this Agreement, to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project, and any Party can cease to continue such discussions at any time. The Agreement does grant Recipient a right of first refusal for any related projects undertaken during the term of this agreement, however The Agreement does not grant Recipient any exclusivity, and Discloser and its Affiliates may purchase similar services, works or goods from other suppliers.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'This Agreement shall not constitute any rights or obligations...to continue discussions or further to enter into any contract regarding the Project'. The changed text then adds 'The Agreement does grant Recipient a right of first refusal for any related projects undertaken during the term of this agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction, as the first statement denies any obligation, while the second confers a specific right of first refusal. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that the change itself creates the in-text contradiction.
